theme: Give the office a memory — let it react to what happened, not just what's happening
visual_observations: Desktop: after-hours vibe active, starfield floor overlay looks great, door locked, candle on manager desk. All review #50 improvements live (reduced-motion, focus rings, away signs, idle micro-bubbles). Conference table ambient labels working. Ficus at seedling stage. Mobile: characters still crowd below 480px but the scale fix from #50 helps. The office feels rich in the moment but has no sense of history — every refresh is a blank slate.

ideas.title: Activity Feed Toast Notifications
ideas.description: When a new entry lands in activity.json (worker completes, clocks in/out), show a subtle toast notification at the bottom of the office view that slides up and fades after 4s. Like a Slack notification in the corner. WHY: Right now the activity log only shows in the dashboard tab — if you're watching the office tab you miss everything. A toast bridges both views and makes the office feel alive with real-time events. The App.jsx already polls activity.json — just surface those deltas as toasts.

ideas.title: Worker Task Progress Tooltip on Desk Monitor
ideas.description: Hover over a working character's desk monitor to see a mini progress card: task name, progress %, and time on task. Right now you can only see this by hovering the character avatar itself (char-hover-card), but the monitor is a bigger hover target and semantically makes more sense — you'd look at someone's screen to see what they're working on. WHY: Bigger hit target, more intuitive interaction, and the monitor already has role-colored glow — extending it with content makes it feel purposeful rather than decorative.

ideas.title: Coffee Counter — Track Total Coffees Consumed
ideas.description: Add a tiny counter on the coffee corner: '☕ 47 served'. Increment it each time a worker transitions from working→idle (they earned a coffee). Persist in workers.json or a separate stats field. The number grows over time and becomes a fun office stat. WHY: It's a tiny detail that makes the office feel like it has continuity. People love watching numbers go up. It costs almost nothing to implement — just a counter in state and a small DOM element.

ideas.title: Keyboard Shortcut Overlay — Press '?' to See Controls
ideas.description: The office has clickable characters and tab navigation, but no discoverability. Add a '?' keyboard shortcut that shows a small overlay: Tab = cycle characters, Enter = ping, 1/2 = switch tabs, R = force refresh. WHY: Power users (devs watching their own dashboard) want keyboard control. It's a 30-line component and makes the dashboard feel like a real app, not just a pretty page.

ideas.title: Vibe Streak Counter Visible in Office
ideas.description: The vibeStreak prop drives the ficus growth but the number itself is invisible. Show it as a small badge near the ficus: '🔥 12 streak'. When streak breaks, show a brief '💔 streak lost' animation. WHY: The ficus growth stages are too slow to notice (every 5 streaks). A visible counter gives immediate feedback and creates a 'gamification' hook — people want to keep the streak going.

ideas.title: Inter-Character Chat Bubbles During Idle
ideas.description: When 2+ characters are idle and their wander paths bring them close (within 15% x/y), show a brief chat bubble exchange between them. Pre-written snippets like 'Did you see the latest deploy?' / 'Carlos broke prod again' / 'Maya's CSS is *chef's kiss*'. WHY: This is the holy grail of office life simulation — characters having relationships. It transforms idle time from 'nothing happening' to 'social dynamics'. Medium effort because it needs proximity detection in the wander animation, but the payoff is massive personality injection.

ideas.title: Sound Design — Optional Ambient Office Audio
ideas.description: A toggle (muted by default) that plays subtle ambient office sounds: keyboard clicks when workers are typing, a coffee machine gurgle at the coffee corner, a gentle hum. Use Web Audio API with tiny samples (<50KB total). WHY: Sound is the missing sense. Visual-only dashboards are cool but adding optional audio transforms it into an experience. The 'muted by default' respects users, but the option to turn it on makes the office feel real. This is a large effort but would be a massive differentiator.
